65:
Alex has spent three days deep in a new AI tool and walked into the studio looking like a conspiracy theorist. What he built in minutes would have taken a research team days. Troy is still getting lost on trail runs without water. This episode is part gear talk, part existential crisis, and part proof that the future just arrived whether you're ready or not.

In this episode:
- Troy's been getting lost on trail runs and nearly dying of dehydration in the bush
- The full breakdown on running hydration gear and why you should never buy Runly
- Enrique Zapata smashed the 24-hour pull-up world record with 12,345 reps
- A family at a Sydney steakhouse pulled armpit hair onto their food to dodge a $600 bill
- Alex discovers AI agents and loses his mind over what they can do
- Matt Schumer's viral article about AI replacing white-collar jobs in 6-12 months
- Why tradesmen might be the last ones standing in the AI revolution
- Operators who watch you fix something and then destroy it trying to copy you
- The Norwegian Olympic biathlete who confessed to cheating on live TV
- Tim Caprazac confirmed as the next guest with a 12-week training plan for Troy

64:
Alex goes off on anonymous accounts, Troy redeems himself on the DRSABCD test, and they argue about whether your missus can have a male best friend. Plus a burpee broad jump marathon that took eight days, the top five things that make them irrationally angry, and a full electrical breakdown on why cheap LED lights flicker and why touching a live cable at 50 hertz can kill you.

In this episode:
- Dog act of the week: blokes who talk shit online behind faceless accounts and never put their name to it
- Harry of the week: Man Sharma completing the world's first burpee broad jump marathon in 197 hours
- Can someone in a relationship have a best friend of the opposite sex without it being a problem?
- The Kevin Hart cheating lesson on never putting yourself in positions you shouldn't be in
- Top 5 things that make you irrationally angry including Facebook Marketplace sellers, bin lids, and parking wars
- Cable ties vs zip ties and why calling them the wrong name marks you as low IQ
- A deep dive into LED flicker, AC frequency, and why your body runs at the same hertz as Australian electricity
- Troy finally nails the DRSABCD after embarrassing himself last episode
